title: Crew Log 9-0-1
text: We lost contact with the relay. Strange readings on the sensors. Crew morale is low. Some systems are offline.

title: Crew Log 3-2-7
text: Engineering reports intermittent power surges in sector 3. We patched the conduit but the alarms keep returning.

title: Personal Log - J. Morales
text: I keep dreaming of static. The cameras show nothing but noise. I don't know if it's the ship or me.

title: Bridge Log 1
text: Unidentified impact on the hull. External cameras corrupted. We sealed the breach but pressure readings are unstable.

title: Medical Note
text: Supplies are dwindling. Medbay is rationing analgesics. If this continues, morale will drop further.

title: Engineering Report
text: The anomaly in the engine room is growing. It's emitting a low hum and causing power fluctuations. We can't get close to it.

title: Crew Log 5-4-2
text: The ship's AI is acting erratically. It keeps repeating the same message about 'the void'. We're not sure if it's a glitch or something else.

title: Personal Log - S. Lee
text: I found something in the cargo bay. A strange object covered in residue. It feels like it's pulsing. I don't want to touch it again.

title: Bridge Log 2
text: The anomaly is affecting our navigation systems. We can't plot a course out of here. We're drifting aimlessly.

title: Medical Note - Dr. Kline
text: Several crew members are showing symptoms of nausea and headaches. I suspect it's related to the anomaly, but I can't be sure without proper equipment.

name: captain_log_1
title: Captain’s Log – First Anomaly
content: We detected a signal outside all known frequency bands. Navigation reports a temporal offset of ±0.4 seconds. I’ve ordered the Eidolon module isolated until we understand what’s happening. If the drift increases, we abort the experiment.

name: engineering_log_1
title: Engineering Report – Core Instability
content: Quantum core is resonating with its own future state. Systems revert to earlier configurations—doors locking themselves, lights resetting. Time inside the ship is no longer consistent. I recommend immediate evacuation.

name: medbay_log_1
title: Medical Log – Crew Symptoms
content: Crew reports intrusive memories, auditory hallucinations of their own voices, and the sensation of being observed. Symptoms are not psychological. Temporal interference is affecting cognitive stability. If drift continues, identity loss is inevitable.

name: science_log_1
title: Science Log – Eidolon Reacts
content: The module is rewriting its own diagnostics. Access codes fail without reason. It is transmitting signals we never programmed. I can’t tell if this is a malfunction… or intent.

name: captain_log_2
title: Captain’s Log – Final Entry
content: Evacuation ordered. Some crew stayed behind to shut down the module. I heard my own voice over the intercom—older, strained—warning me not to deactivate Eidolon. 'If you shut it down, it happens again.' If you’re reading this… you’re not the first.

name: crew_fragment_1
title: Crew Note – Lost Time
content: I went to sleep at 22:00. Woke up at 21:47. My own log entry for tomorrow was already written. I don’t remember writing it.

name: engineering_fragment_1
title: Engineering Fragment – Echo
content: Audio log: footsteps in the corridor. Pause. Then the same footsteps again… but reversed, like time folding back on itself.
